label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3

extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won a second consecutive sprint via van Poppel's late leadout, beating Pedersen after Ackermann faded from launching 750m out
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo dominated pace-setting all day but couldn't convert work into positioning at final turn 750m out
Ackermann's premature launch before 750m marker allowed fresher sprinters to slipstream then overtake in final 300m
Van Poppel's repeated late-release leadout for Bennett proves more effective than earlier train positioning in flat sprints

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: back-to-back sprint wins suggest form trajectory improving rather than peaking,van Poppel chemistry decisive
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: consistently second indicates positioning skill but lacks acceleration to beat Bennett when both have clean run
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: premature acceleration before 750m reveals poor sprint timing despite apparent form
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: out of position finish suggests either team dysfunction or inability to navigate technical sprint finishes
rider_signals.AFFINI Edoardo: neutral: inheriting red jersey within Jumbo-Visma indicates team's TTT strength without individual effort
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: negative: crash with Einhorn ending race signals vulnerability in nervous flat stages despite climbing focus
